questions.question: A line passes through the points (−2, 5) and (3, −10). What is the slope of the line?
questions.explanation: Use the slope formula: m = (y₂ − y₁)/(x₂ − x₁) = (−10 − 5)/(3 − (−2)) = −15/5 = −3.

questions.question: A line with slope 2/5 passes through (10, 8). Write the equation in the form y = mx + b.
Two perpendicular lines intersect at (4, 3). One line has slope 1/2. What is the slope of the other line?
questions.explanation: Perpendicular slopes are negative reciprocals. If m₁ = 1/2, then m₂ = −2.

questions.question: A line passes through (−1, 4) and (a, 10), where the slope is 3. Find the value of a.
The line 3x − 4y = 12 is transformed by shifting it up 5 units. What is the y-intercept of the new line?
questions.explanation: Original: 3x − 4y = 12 → y = (3/4)x − 3, so y-intercept is −3. Shift up 5: −3 + 5 = 2. Wait: check work. Rewrite: −4y = −3x + 12 → y = (3/4)x − 3. Shift up 5: new y-intercept = −3 + 5 = 2. Actually: −4y = −3x + 12, divide by −4: y = 0.75x − 3. Plus 5 gives y = 0.75x + 2. So answer is 2. Recalculating: 3x − 4y = 12 gives −4y = −3x + 12, so y = (3/4)x − 3. Shifted up 5: y = (3/4)x − 3 + 5 = (3/4)x + 2. Y-intercept is 2.

questions.question: A line has equation 2x + 5y = 20. A second line is parallel to this one and passes through (−5, 6). Write the equation of the second line in the form ax + by = c where a, b, c are integers.
questions.explanation: Parallel lines have the same slope. From 2x + 5y = 20, slope is −2/5. Using point (−5, 6): 2(−5) + 5(6) = −10 + 30 = 20. So the equation is 2x + 5y = 20.
